hooks.command: root="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)}"; cat "$root/docs/01_Vault/ProjectTemplate/00_System/Next Session Handoff.md" 2>/dev/null || echo 'No handoff found'

input=$(cat); file_path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -q '\.py$'; then cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)" && (ruff check --fix --quiet -- "$file_path" 2>/dev/null; ruff format --quiet -- "$file_path" 2>/dev/null); fi; exit 0

input=$(cat); file_path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd); cd "$root" || exit 0; if [ ! -f .pre-commit-config.yaml ] || [ -z "$file_path" ]; then exit 0; fi; case "$file_path" in *.md|*.yml|*.yaml) ;; *) exit 0;; esac; pre-commit run --files "$file_path" 2>/dev/null; exit 0

hooks.prompt: If this was a CI/test/lint/build failure, extract the ROOT CAUSE in one sentence and suggest a preventive rule. Reply with suggestion or PASS if not a failure.

LOAD reminder (non-blocking; reply ALLOW): Per docs/00_Core/SESSION_LIFECYCLE.md, before substantive edits have you completed LOAD this session (Next Session Handoff, follow relates_to/indexes for needed subgraph, Current Focus)? If not, read those vault files first. Trivial typo-only edits may skip.

Sensitive file guard: BLOCK edits to .env (but allow .env.example), any path under archive/ if your team treats it as immutable, *.lock, poetry.lock, package-lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, or secrets directories. ALLOW normal source and docs. Reply BLOCK: <reason> or ALLOW.

If the edit touches src/**/*.py, scan the NEW text only for obvious secret patterns: AWS access keys, private keys (BEGIN PRIVATE KEY), and long base64 blobs labeled as tokens. BLOCK if a new secret is being inserted. ALLOW if only references to env vars or already-redacted examples.
If the bash command would commit or push to protected branch main/master, BLOCK. Patterns: git commit on main/master, git push on main/master, git push origin HEAD:main or :master. Otherwise ALLOW.

SAVE per docs/00_Core/SESSION_LIFECYCLE.md before stopping. Verify: (1) Next Session Handoff.md updated (resume, shipped, remains, blockers). (2) Vault: new/updated small linked nodes with valid relates_to where needed; not only monolithic edits. (3) Current Focus.md updated if branch, PR, or focus changed. (4) On failure: handoff records what failed and links. Then also: tests/CI adequate? shortcuts? patterns to remember? Rate session completeness 1-10; if <7, flag gaps.
